XOnlineMessageDownloadAttachmentToMemory

Downloads an attachment to memory.

HRESULT XOnlineMessageDownloadAttachmentToMemory(
    XONLINETASK_HANDLE hDetailsTask,
    WORD wPropTag,
    PBYTE pbBuffer,
    DWORD dwBufferSize,
    HANDLE hWorkEvent,
    PXONLINETASK_HANDLE phDownloadTask
);

Parameters

hDetailsTask
[in] Task handle obtained from XOnlineMessageDetails.
wPropTag
[in] Unique identifier of the attachment.
pbBuffer
[in, out] Pointer to the memory block into which the attachment is downloaded.
dwBufferSize
[in] Size, in bytes, of the buffer specified by the pbBuffer parameter.
hWorkEvent
[in, optional] Handle of the event you want triggered every time there is work to be performed on this task. If you do not want to assign an event, set this parameter to NULL.
phDownloadTask
[out] Receives a handle to the download task. This handle can be passed to XOnlineMessageDownloadAttachmentGetProgress to report the progress of the download.

Return Values

Returns S_OK if successful, an error code otherwise.

Remarks

A property tag specifies the type of the property in its most significant byte. Use the XOnlineMessageGetPropType macro to retrieve it. Before calling the XOnlineMessageDownloadAttachmentToMemory function, ensure that the property type is XONLINE_MSG_PROP_TYPE_ATTACHMENT. Also, call the XOnlineMessageDetailsGetResultsProperty function to ensure that its pdwAttachmentFlags parameter contains the value XONLINE_MSG_ATTACHMENT_FLAG_DIRECTORY and its return type is XONLINE_E_MESSAGE_PROPERTY_DOWNLOAD_REQUIRED.

The memory block pointed to by pbBuffer must remain valid until the download completes.

This function is intended for smaller downloads such as voice messages that just get stored in memory and never get written to disk. This function does not have a maximum size for downloads except that it is constrained by the size of the buffer passed into the function.

The integrity of the data is verified by the function prior to being returned to the caller. However, the downloaded data is returned as-is without additional security features. Titles are not recommended to save such downloaded to disk without additional security measures.

Only one download-to-memory operation can be performed at a time.

Requirements

Header: Declared in Xonline.h.

Import Library: Use Xonline.lib.

See Also

XOnlineMessageDetails, XOnlineMessageDownloadAttachmentGetProgress
